title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: A coronary angioplasty procedure is also known as percutaneous coronary intervention. The procedure is done on blood vessels called coronary arteries. They supply your heart muscle with oxygen. The goal is to restore blood flow if a substance called plaque has significantly narrowed these vessels.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: To begin the procedure, the doctor will numb the skin in your groin. A needle will be placed through your skin and into the femoral artery.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: Next, a flexible guide wire will be passed through the needle into your artery.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: Then, the needle will be withdrawn. It will be exchanged for a small, flexible tube called a sheath. This permits access into your artery. You may feel pressure when the doctor inserts the sheath, but you will not feel it moving inside your artery.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: Next, the guide wire will be advanced up to your heart. A flexible tube called a catheter will be advanced over the wire to your coronary arteries. The progress of the procedure will be checked with an x-ray device called a flora scope.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: At this point, your doctor will remove the guide wire. Then, the doctor will move the tip of the catheter just inside the coronary artery to be examined.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: A special die will be injected into the artery. This allows your doctor to view it better with a flora scope. The die will make any blockages in the artery stand out.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: If a significant blockage is found, your doctor will insert a guide wire into the artery. A balloon on the tip of the catheter will be moved along the wire to the blockage.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: When the balloon inflates, it will expand the artery and improve the blood flow. You may feel some chest discomfort while this is happening.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: After this, your doctor will deflate and remove the balloon.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: A wire mesh tube called a stent may be placed in the treated area. The stent helps keep the coronary artery open. Your doctor will choose the proper size stent, which is compressed over a balloon.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: The stent will be moved into the artery over the same guide wire. When the balloon is inflated, the stent will expand and lock into place.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: After the balloon catheter is taken out, the stent will stay in place to hold the artery At the end of the procedure, the guide wire will be removed.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
text_en: To find out more about coronary artery angioplasty, talk to your healthcare provider.
